effects.gear: Phone on a locked-off tripod; even lighting.
effects.technique_note: Ingested from a public Instagram reel via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription. Same locked-off cloning method as the vault 'cloning' family, but uses CapCut's Auto Background Removal instead of manual masking.
filming_steps: I'm going to show you how to do the cloning effect.
Actually, she's going to show you how to do the cloning effect.
You'll need a clip of yourself sitting in a chair, pointing to your clones and stepping out.
You'll need a second clip of you stepping into the frame sitting down in the chair.
editing_steps: Open up CapCut and import both of your clips.
Scroll to the second clip where you're coming into the frame.
Select your clip and in your editing tool, select Overlay.
Drag your second clip underneath your first clip where you're pointing to your clone.
Select your first clip and in your editing tool, select Duplicate and in your editing tools, scroll to Overlay, select Overlay.
Drag that overlay right underneath the first clip.
In your editing tools, scroll to Remove Background, select Auto Removal.
This removes the background of the first clip, allowing you to see your clone from the second clip.

lessons.transcript: I'm going to show you how to do the cloning effect. Actually, she's going to show you how to do the cloning effect. Let's go. effects.gear: Phone; room with space to walk across frame.
effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Film one clip of yourself walking across the frame, side to side (locked-off camera).
editing_steps: Add your text (Text → Add text) and make it large.
Select the video clip and Duplicate it.
Move the duplicate to an Overlay layer; line the two identical clips up exactly on top of each other.
On the overlay, go to Mask → Horizontal, then rotate the mask line so it runs vertically.
Drag the mask line all the way over onto you and add a keyframe.
Keep adding keyframes so the mask line follows you as you walk across the frame.
Select the Text → go to Layers and swap the order so the overlay sits ABOVE the text.
Result: the text appears behind you as you walk past it.

effects.effect: Flying Object via Printed Sticker + Green Paper
effects.gear: Football, printer + paper, a sheet of green paper, tape, phone.
effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Film yourself throwing a football across the field.
Make an Instagram sticker of the object, print it, and cut it out.
Cut a hole in a sheet of green paper; tape the sticker behind the hole and push it through so you can hold it from behind.
Film the front of the green paper while pulling the sticker through it.
editing_steps: In Instagram, open a green story, recreate the sticker, and screenshot it.
Import into CapCut in order: throwing clip → green-paper clip → sticker screenshot.
Select the screenshot → Remove Background → Chroma Key → pick the green → set as Overlay and pull it to the front (now the sticker floats in the air).
Select the green-paper clip → Remove Background → Chroma Key → green → Overlay; drag it to the end of the screenshot clip.
At the start of the paper clip add a keyframe; scroll ahead and resize/position the paper over the ball; repeat so the paper follows the ball.
Export.

lessons.transcript: First, film yourself throwing a football across the field. Next go to Instagram and create a sticker like this. Then print this out on a piece of paper and cut it out. Take a green paper and cut a hole in the middle. Put some tape on the back of your sticker and push it through the hole so you can grab it from the other side. Then film the front while pulling it through the paper. In Instagram, open a green story and create that sticker again. Take a screenshot and open it all in the cap cut app. First the clip of you throwing, then the green paper clip and then the screenshot. Select the screenshot, hit remove background and chroma key. Now select the green area and then hit overlay. This will drop it to a second layer and now pull it all the way to the front of the track. You now have the sticker in the air. Select the green paper clip, hit remove background chroma key and select the green area. Again hit overlay and drag it to the end of that screenshot clip right here. The clip now looks like this. Scroll to the beginning of the paper clip and hit keyframe. Now scroll a little bit ahead in the clip and resize and place the paper over the ball. Do the same thing again, scroll ahead and place the paper over the ball. The paper now follows the ball, all you have to do is export the video and that's it.

effects.gear: Phone; your main video clip; a white-screen image.
effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Have your main video clip ready (the shot the 3D text will sit in).
editing_steps: Open CapCut and upload a plain white screen image (find one on Google).
Add your text in a bold color (red or yellow). Export the clip, then screenshot the text from your Photos.
Back in CapCut, upload your main video. Tap Overlay, add the text screenshot on top, and crop it.
Remove Background → Chroma Key → select the white → leaves just the colored text.
Effects → Video Effects → 'Player 3'; turn OFF glow and texture.

effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Shoot a landscape clip (turn the phone sideways).
On camera, tap three times then jump three times — these motions are your sync points.
editing_steps: Import into CapCut; set Aspect Ratio to 9:16.
Add three separate Text layers; set each to static/'lasting'.
Scroll to just before the first foot tap and tap Keyframe; scroll one frame forward to the tap and drag the video down.
Repeat for each tap/jump, revealing the text gradually after each movement.
Highlight the video layer → Duplicate → Overlay → drag underneath the original.
Tap Layers → Front. Done.

lessons.transcript: This video got 3.5 million views. Here's how to easily make it with your phone. First thing you're gonna do is get a landscape shot. For those of you don't know, that means turning your phone like that. All you got to do is just tap three times and then jump three times. And now on to the edit. Import your shot into cap cut. Tap aspect ratio and 9 by 16. Then tap add text and add three layers of text and tap lasting text for each layer. Scroll to right before your first foot tap. Tap the keyframe button then scroll one-frame forward when your foot taps and drag the video down. Repeat this for the remaining foot taps and jumps. Position your text to be gradually revealed after each video movement. Highlight your video layer, tap duplicate, overlay and drag it directly underneath the original clip. Tap layers, tap front and you're done. You should now have this.

effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Break your script into 3 parts.
Shot 1 (wide): walk toward the camera; at a set point, turn your head to the left.
Shot 2 (close-up): deliver the next part, then walk out of frame.
Shot 3 (medium): walk into frame for the final part.
editing_steps: Cut the three shots together in sequence; the matched movement (head turn / walking in and out) hides the cuts as a match cut.

lessons.title: Video by juliagaom
lessons.transcript: So if you're filming talking head videos that nobody wants to watch, have your videographer try this tip. You need to break up your scripts. For the first shot, you're gonna film yourself in a wide shot, walking towards a cameraman. And then when you reach a certain point, you're gonna turn your head to the left. Now shoot the second part of your script in a close up shot. Once you're done, walk out of the frame. For the third part of your script, you can film yourself in a medium shot walking into the frame. Let's start it again. My name is Julia and my team and I run a short form content agency, where we script edit and post content for a remote fitness and online business coaches. If you're an established entrepreneur and want more information, just comment info to start a conversation.

source_meta.caption:
When scripting for clients, we’ll often have them change scenes throughout the video because even if the information is valuable, attention spans on short form content are still short 🕥

Small scene changes, movement, and visual variation help keep the viewer engaged while the person is speaking.

So when you or your team are scripting short form content, it’s good to intentionally build in these little scene changes and visual resets to help maintain attention throughout the video 👀🍬

effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Record a clip facing the camera, holding a phone in one hand.
With your free hand, swipe up from the screen as if pulling the display into the air, then keep swiping like scrolling a floating screen.
Separately, screen-record yourself scrolling on the phone.
editing_steps: Import both into CapCut; set the screen recording as an Overlay on layer 2 at ~50% opacity.
Add a keyframe where you swipe the hologram up; at the start, position the screen recording just below the phone.
Duplicate your main clip → move to layer 3 → add a Horizontal mask lined up at the top edge of the phone, with a slight feather.
Tweak timing so the hologram rises exactly with your swipe; match the scrolling to your finger movement; trim and export.

lessons.title: Video by harry__allsop
lessons.transcript: Here's how to turn your phone into a hologram. Give me 60 seconds and I'll show you how. First, record a clip of you facing the camera, holding a phone in one hand. With your free hand, swipe up from the screen like you're pulling the display out into the air. Then keep swiping as if you're scrolling a floating screen. Next, take a screen recording of you scrolling on your phone and put both clips into CapCut. But screen recording is an overlay. Move it to layer two and lower the opacity to about 50%. Add a keyframe where you swipe the hologram up, then go back to the start and position the screen recording just below the phone. Duplicate your main clip, move it to layer three and add a horizontal mask and line it up at the top edge of the phone with a slight feather. Now just tweak the timing so the hologram rises out exactly with your swipe. Match the scrolling to your finger movement, trim the clips to match and hit export. And that's it. Send this to a friend who needs better edits and follow some more effects like this.

filming_steps: Film your main clip talking to the camera.
Film a clean plate of the exact same scene (empty, camera unmoved).
editing_steps: Import both into CapCut; select your talking clip → Overlay → move to the start of layer 2.
Trim the start of the talking clip slightly; scrub forward a few frames → Split.
Select the first section → Effects → Body Effects → search 'Flash' → White Flash.
Remove Background (Auto or Custom) to cut yourself out. Done.

effects.gear: Phone (iPhone/rig helps for smooth slow-mo jump).
effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription.
filming_steps: Film a foreground shot of you.
Film a jump (a 'set jump'), ideally on an iPhone/rig for smooth slow motion.
editing_steps: Import the foreground clip; add the jumping clip as an Overlay.
Select the jumping clip → Remove Background; Speed → slow it down.
Scale the cutout down and position it where you want.
Add keyframes at the start and a few frames forward; on the second keyframe pull it up slightly for the floating effect.
Repeat for additional clones; to reveal the main/angel subject, import the foreground clip as an overlay and Remove Background.

lessons.transcript: Let's recreate this, this is the top core. First, take a shot of you in the foreground, then record a set jump in an iPhone rig to get a smooth slow motion effect, input the foreground clip and add the jumping clip as an overlay, select the jumping clip, click on remove background, tap on speed and reduce the speed as desired, take the cutout, scale it down and position it wherever you want. Add keyframes at the beginning and also a few frames forward. On second keyframe, pull it up a little bit to get the floating effect. Now repeat the same process for the other clips as well, to appear the angel subject, import the foreground clip as an overlay and remove background, there you have it.

effects.technique_note: Ingested from an external Instagram video via yt-dlp + ffmpeg frames + Whisper transcription. Steps auto-split (review recommended).
filming_steps: How to do this?
Start by filming a video where your jacket falls down in front of you.
Leave the jacket where it is, walk out of the frame and keep recording for a few seconds.
Open this clip in
editing_steps: Make one cut when the jacket starts to fall.
One when it's on the floor and the last one when you are out of the frame.
Select the section when you walk out and delete it.
Next, select the last clip, hit overlay and drag it over the clip where the jacket falls down.

filming_steps: Here's how to make this effect record a clip of yourself swiping up on a surface like a wall as if you're adjusting brightness and take a screen recording of your phone's brightness bar over a green background.
Open the screen recording and trim the ends keeping only the part where the brightness bar moves.
Crop it down so only the brightness bar is visible.
editing_steps: Import both clips into cap cut.
Select the brightness bar hit overlay and move it to the second layer.
Find the exact point where your hand touches the wall and align the brightness bar with this moment.
With the brightness bar clip still selected go to chroma key and use the color picker to select the green background.
Resize and move the brightness bar so it follows the motion of your hand.
Add a freeze frame at the start of the brightness bar clip and extend it to just before your hand starts moving.
Trim an adjust the speed of the brightness bar to match the swipe speed in your main clip.
Add another freeze frame at the end of the brightness bar clip.
Duplicate your main clip hit overlay and move it to the third layer.
Use remove background on this duplicate to create a cut out of yourself.
Go to adjust and lower some of the sliders to make it look like night time.

Go to the point your hand is about to move upwards and hit keyframe and then go to the point where your hand is right at the top of the slider and set another keyframe and go back to the adjust and increase those sliders back to their normal values.
That's it your video should look like this.

Open the cup cup app and add your text or image.

This part is very important because anything you do inside the compound clip will only affect that element and not your entire video.
Open the compound clip, go to effect search for player 3 and drag it directly on top of your text or image.
Click on the effect, set text shot to 0 so that this image disappears.
Use the rotate slider to match the perspective to your shot.

You can even add blur or other effects inside the compound clip and it only affects that element.
I guess this is your sign to start playing around with compound clips.
